title: Relief Food Distribution
tagline: Nutritional support for malnourished children aged 5 and below — identified and assessed through our Community Clinic.
intro_p1: Malnutrition in early childhood has lifelong consequences — stunting physical growth, impairing brain development, and reducing a child's ability to learn and thrive. In the low-income communities of Kisumu, many families simply cannot provide adequate nutrition for their young children.
intro_p2: UCDP's Relief Food Distribution programme targets children aged 5 years and below who are malnourished and vulnerable within the community. Rather than a blanket distribution, we take a clinical approach: children are assessed through our Community Clinic, where health workers identify and confirm cases of malnutrition before food relief is provided.
intro_p3: This ensures that our limited resources reach those who need them most, and that each child's nutritional progress can be monitored over time.

quick_facts.label: What We Provide
quick_facts.value: Food packages for families in need

steps.title: Clinical Assessment
steps.text: Children are brought to our Community Clinic where health workers assess and confirm malnutrition before any food relief is given.

steps.title: Identification of Beneficiaries
steps.text: Assessed and confirmed malnourished children aged 5 and below are registered as beneficiaries.

steps.title: Food Relief Distribution
steps.text: Food packages are provided to the families of registered children, free of charge.

steps.title: Follow-Up & Monitoring
steps.text: We follow up regularly through the clinic to monitor each child's nutritional recovery and adjust support accordingly.
